title: vandellia scapigera
intro: Vandellia scapigera, also known as Kanta or Kanta Mora, is a plant species that belongs to the family Loasaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine and has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Vandellia scapigera include its ability to treat various skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
